title: Which of the following refers to the sum of processes performed by a pharmacist from reading, validating, and interpreti…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Which of the following refers to the sum of processes performed by a pharmacist from reading, validating, and interpreting prescriptions; preparing; packaging; labeling; record keeping; dose calculations; and counseling or giving information, in relation to the sale or transfer of pharmaceutical products, with or without a prescription or medication order?


based on ph pharmacy law which is more correct dispensing or compounding

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Dispensing refers to the sum of processes performed by a pharmacist from reading, validating, and interpreting prescriptions; preparing; packaging; labeling; record keeping; dose calculations; and counseling or giving information, in relation to the sale or transfer of pharmaceutical products, with or without a prescription or medication order. 

Based on PH Pharmacy Law, both dispensing and compounding are important functions of a pharmacist and must be carried out in accordance with established standards and guidelines. However, dispensing is more commonly used in day-to-day pharmacy practice compared to compounding, which involves the preparation of customized medications in accordance with specific patient needs.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Which of the following refers to any act of 
introducing any dangerous drug into the body of 
any person, with or without his/her knowledge, by 
injection, inhalation, ingestion or other means?
a. Dispense
b. Administer
c. Cultivate or Culture
d. Use
e. Deliver
f. Chemical Diversion
g. Manufacture
h. Controlled Delivery
